seoTitle: How to Add a Logo in Brevo
metaDescription: Upload your company logo into Brevo's Brand Library or directly inside individual newsletters, with proper sizing, links, and alt text.
intro: Wrestling with stretched image placeholders or manually replacing branding graphics across every template wastes time. Brevo's centralized Brand Library and drag-and-drop media blocks let you lock in your logo once and have it distribute consistently across new email designs. This guide covers uploading and positioning it correctly.

steps.heading: Open your design canvas
steps.body: Launch the layout canvas for the campaign or Brand Library setting where you want to add the logo.

steps.heading: Position header rows and logo blocks
steps.body: Drag and arrange fresh header rows and logo blocks inside the layout grid where the logo should appear.

steps.heading: Upload your media file
steps.body: Upload a crisp PNG or vector version of your logo, avoiding low-resolution files that will look pixelated.

steps.heading: Manage display aspect ratios
steps.body: Adjust the responsive display sizing so the logo maintains its proportions across desktop and mobile views.

steps.heading: Add link paths and alt text
steps.body: Inject a custom hyperlink target so the logo links back to your website, and set alternative text parameters for email client accessibility.

faq.question: Should I use PNG or vector files for my logo?
faq.answer: Crisp PNG or vector files both work well, producing sharp results without pixelation compared to low-resolution image formats.

faq.question: Can I make my logo clickable?
faq.answer: Yes, you can inject a custom hyperlink target so clicking the logo takes recipients to your website.

faq.question: Does adding a logo to the Brand Library update past campaigns?
faq.answer: No, Brand Library updates generally apply to new templates and mockups going forward, not previously sent campaigns.

faq.question: Why does my logo look distorted on mobile?
faq.answer: This usually means the aspect ratio settings weren't adjusted for responsive display, so check the sizing before sending.
